content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 30, 2002

Eric F. Galen
Paul, Hastings, Janofsky & Walker, LLP
515 South Flower, 25th Floor
Los Angeles, CA 90071-2228

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No.: W-02-265

Dear Mr. Galen:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act") sent to the Commission's Executive Director on September 20, 2002. As we discussed in our telephone conversation on September 5, 2002, the Commission does not provide confirmation of telephone advice. Telephone advice, as informal assistance, does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice (section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3)).

Consequently, we assume your letter dated September 20, 2002, is a request for written advice pertaining to your question about the duties of public agencies to update their conflict of interest codes. In future, these requests for written advice should be mailed to the attention of the Legal Division of the Commission in order to avoid any delays in obtaining a response.

The Act provides that the Commission may decline to provide written advice under certain circumstances. Two of these apply in this context:

(1) Under the provisions of regulation 18329(b)(8)(B), unless you identify the person in question on whose behalf you have requested the advice, and state that you are that person's authorized representative, the Commission may decline to address your questions or provide analysis.

(2) Additionally, since you have not named a person subject to the Act that you are authorized to represent, it appears that your inquiries are hypothetical in nature. Under regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), the Commission may decline to provide written advice in response to hypothetical questions.

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear in Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-02-265
Page No. 2

I enclose a copy of regulation 18329 and a publication entitled, "Getting Advice
from the FPPC." If you should need specific written advice in the future, please consult
the booklet for the process used to request such advice.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [signature] ___
John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

Enclosures
JWW:jg
